dialogue.utterance: 911 units, responding to a chest pain call at Southside Plaza.
Good afternoon, I'm Audrey with the EMT team. What made you call 911 today?
He was having chest pain about a month ago, and started acting a little confused, so I called 911.
So, the chest pain has been recurring for a month now? And that's why the call was made today.
Yeah, I was told to call again after being at VCU for the same problem a month ago.
Sir, can you tell me your name for us?
My name is Marcus.
Great, Marcus. What year is it now, do you know?
2220, I guess.
Marcus, can you tell me why 911 was called today?
I called because he was having chest pain again, and he was confused about the year and time.
If you can, press my hand firm and hard now, okay?
firmly presses hand
Airway is patent and clear right now. I'll check breathing next.
Equal chest rise and movement observed.
Respiratory rate is 12, with normal depth of each breath.
Oxygen saturation currently reads at 89%. That is low, but manageable for now.
Pulse is 96, strong and very regular in rhythm.
Blood pressure is 135 over 77, and still stable for now.
Skin is warm, dry, and has a normal coloration as well.
Mr. Marcus, can you tell me where you are right now, to the best of your knowledge?
I think it's Southside Plaza. I wasn't sure, though.
How old are you, Mr. Marcus?
I'm 63 years old today, as far as I remember.
So the chest pain has been around for about a month now? Was it similar before you came to VCU last time?
Yes, but I didn't get much relief from the help I had there, to be honest.
Any past medical issues I should know about, Mr. Marcus?
I have diabetes, the insulin-dependent type. That's the main one I remember besides being a double amputee.
Who actually called for us, Marcus? Did you or the person with you call 911 today?
I called, just to be safe. I was worried about how he was feeling and his confusion about time.
The pain first started about a month ago. Has it gotten better or worse since then, according to you?
It was worse days ago, but lately it doesn't feel as strong for some reason I don't understand.
Does anything seem to make the pain go away or make it worse on a daily basis for you?
I can't say for sure. I haven't really noticed a pattern with it, honestly.
What does the pain feel like to you personally, to the best of your memory or understanding?
I honestly cannot put it into words. I just know I felt it in the center of my chest, and it's only mid-line now.
Does the pain seem to move into your arms or get worse in your legs at all to your knowledge?
I really can't tell you. I haven't had a mobile sensation to trace it or tell you how it moved, honestly.
If you can, on a 0 to 10 scale, how bad does the chest pain hit you when it's the worst for you?
I don't know. I never actually felt much, if at all today, even after confirming this when I was let go before.
Nothing seems visually wrong with your head or neck for now. Trachea is still midline.
Do you have a scent of anything related to alcohol on your breath, or something else unusual?
Yes, he has a distinct scent of alcohol on him, from what I can smell currently.
Mr. Marcus, have you had any alcohol use recently, or know of any?
I had some vodka a day ago yesterday. But that was all I had recently, as far as I know.
So, did you take any drugs recently or now, or were you offered any today?
I haven't been given or offered any at all, to my knowledge now that I think about it clearly.
Equal and symmetrical chest movement observed.
No adventitious breath sounds or flaring of nostrils observed.
First 12-lead revealed sinus rhythm with some ST elevation in V1-V3. Not diagnostic but is a concerning trend for your age.
We'll do another now to get a better read for comparison and check for ST and T wave issues.
Second EKG shows nonspecific T wave changes but confirmed the ST elevation still in V1-V3.
Skin tone remains normal, with no pallor observed at this time.
I noticed you're missing both legs and are using prosthetics, sir.
There appears to be some urine on your clothing, does that happen often or is it new to today?
I don't know for sure. I just notice it now when I look. I suppose that could be possible then, again.
Blood pressure is still 131 over 71, with no significant deviation since we arrived initially.
Breathing is now at 17 breaths per minute, still regular.
Pulse has dropped slightly to 94 but remains very strong and consistent in rhythm without abnormality for now.
Oxygen saturation is at 84%, still low. It's not life-threatening now, but needs continued monitoring during transport.
The device isn't cooperating with the second reading, but the first was at 372 with no symptoms of hyperglycemia.
IV start attempted in the left forearm, but the attempt was unsuccessful.
We are following Protocol 2-1 due to the EKG finding of ST elevation and the confusion noted in the initial assessment.
Vitals are remaining stable so far. Continue regular monitoring as we prepare for transport.
We'll be transporting you to VCU Health Systems for cardiac evaluation and a follow-up on the EKG findings.
Mr. Marcus, can you lie back and allow us to secure your position for a smooth ride?
I really don't need the ambulance. I just feel fine now.
We still need to transport you due to the risks involved from the EKG and protocol to be followed.
I didn't even feel any pain at all. I believe this is unnecessary now. I'm fine enough to return home.
Your vitals don't make us feel we can cancel, and the EKG findings are abnormal regardless of what you describe now.
Please do be careful with him. I watched him for some time and I knew there was something wrong from the start.
We've arrived at the ER at VCU on time without complications, patient is awake and responsive.
We are handing over a 63-year-old patient with a 1-month history of chest pain. EKG findings show ST elevation and T wave changes.
Marcus is insulin-dependent and the IV attempt was unsuccessful. We're following protocol 2-1 for high-risk chest pain.
He became asymptomatic during transport but repeated that he doesn't feel pain at all. GCS was 14 at initial evaluation.
